prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, nearly black frame. Little visual content is distinguishable, though a faint archive watermark is visible.

By the later scene, the image shows three people seated close together indoors, apparently at a table. On the left is an adult man in a reddish cardigan or jacket over a light shirt; he is turned toward two children. In the center is a young girl wearing a pink or coral top with a darker collar, smiling broadly. On the right is a young boy wearing a striped shirt, also smiling and looking toward the girl. The children appear to be holding or handling small food items or utensils near the bottom of the frame, though the details are not fully clear. The mood conveyed visually is cheerful and familial.

The final frame cuts to a close-up product shot. A hand holds a green can labeled “Chicken of the Sea” with a red panel reading “TUNA.” The can is centered against a plain gray background, making the brand and product text the main visual focus. A faint archive watermark remains visible across the lower part of the image.
